Some Gymnadenia species have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. The bioactive constituents of Gymnadenia species include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which may contribute to their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Gymnadenia species include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
